summary: Unconditional --bind /var/run/docker.sock crashes bwrap when socket absent
failure_scenario: On any system without Docker installed, /var/run/docker.sock does not exist. bwrap --bind requires the source path to exist. Every sandboxed command reaching executeInBwrap will throw an execSync error with a cryptic 'bind mount failed' message. The caller (sandbox-manager.ts execute(), line 82) does not pre-check for the socket.

summary: Shell command string built via naive double-quote interpolation — special characters in writable paths cause execution errors or unintended shell expansion
failure_scenario: If a registered writable path contains $ (e.g., /tmp/$USER/data), backticks, or double quotes, the constructed commandStr passed to execSync() is misinterpreted by the shell. $PATH expands, backticks execute sub-commands. The callers (sandbox-manager.ts registerWritable, line 94) create directories via mkdirSync but neither validate nor sanitize path characters. run-command.ts executeViaSandbox is indirectly affected because it calls mgr.execute().

summary: parseVolumeMounts regex requires whitespace after -v, missing combined short-flag form -v/path:/path — volume mount validation bypass
failure_scenario: A user command like 'docker run -v/etc/shadow:/shadow ubuntu' has no space after -v. The regex (?:-v|--volume)\s+ requires \s+, so this mount is not parsed. isDockerCommand() matches (line 49 of sandbox-manager.ts), enters the validation block, but parseVolumeMounts returns an empty array, validation returns ok:true. The protect-path check is entirely skipped for that mount. Attacker-controlled command strings can mount sensitive host paths into containers.

summary: executeDirect timeout handler discards partial stdout/stderr, inconsistent with duplicated executeDirectly in run-command.ts which captures partial output
failure_scenario: A sandbox-disabled or bwrap-unavailable user runs a long command that produces partial output before timing out. If the call goes through sandbox-manager.ts executeDirect (sandbox disabled, line 45, or bwrap unavailable, lines 67/79), the timeout returns content:'[TRUNCATED: command timed out]' with no partial output. If instead the call goes through run-command.ts executeDirectly (no sandbox manager at all, line 18), partial output IS captured. Users see different timeout behavior depending on initialization path.

summary: isBwrapAvailable() cached at manager creation; executeInBwrap re-checks at runtime and returns exitCode:0 without running command if bwrap missing — ToCToU
failure_scenario: bwrap is present when the sandbox manager is created (line 36), so bwrapAvailable=true. Between creation and execution, bwrap is removed or becomes unavailable. execute() skips the !bwrapAvailable guard (lines 65-80) and calls executeInBwrap (line 82). Inside executeInBwrap, findBwrap() returns null, and the function returns exitCode:0 with a warning message — the command NEVER EXECUTES. User thinks the command succeeded because exitCode is 0.

summary: isDockerCommand only matches docker run/create at string start — sudo, env-var-prefixed, or npx-prefixed docker commands bypass volume validation
failure_scenario: A user command like 'sudo docker run -v /etc/shadow:/shadow ubuntu' or 'DOCKER_HOST=tcp://... docker run -v /etc:/etc ubuntu' does not start with 'docker ', so isDockerCommand() returns false. sandbox-manager.ts execute() skips the entire docker validation block (lines 49-62) and proceeds to bwrap execution without checking volume mounts. The validate() function in createDockerValidator is never called.

summary: WritableRegistration interface with registeredAt field is dead code — never instantiated, never imported, never populated
failure_scenario: The interface declares path: string and registeredAt: number, but no code anywhere creates a WritableRegistration object, imports the type, or sets registeredAt. The actual writable path tracking in path-policy.ts uses Set<string> with no timestamp metadata. Future maintainers might import WritableRegistration expecting registeredAt to be present, but it will always be undefined since nothing populates it.

summary: Volume mount mode regex (ro|rw|z|Z) does not handle comma-separated flags like ro,z or z,ro — read-only enforcement gap
failure_scenario: Docker allows combined mode flags, e.g., -v /host:/container:ro,z. The regex (?:ro|rw|z|Z) matches only a single token. With ro,z the entire mode group fails, mode defaults to 'rw'. While the validator does not currently use the mode field for access decisions, any future enforcement of read-only mounts (e.g., blocking writable mounts to protected paths) would silently treat explicitly read-only mounts as read-write.
